summary: Validated the closing of the two automatic-flow gaps: (A) public quote acceptance with auto-agreement creation, and (B) auto-invoice (draft) creation on public agreement signing when a quote_id is linked. Backend: 8/8 pytest tests pass (test_public_accept_and_auto_invoice.py) covering happy path, idempotency, status rejections (declined/converted), 404 path, regression of auth /quotes/:id/status?status=approved (still creates SA-), auto-invoice on sign with line-items copied + quote_id + agreement_id, sign-twice rejection (no duplicate invoice), and no-invoice creation when agreement has no quote_id. Frontend E2E: full pipeline executed against the public URL — quote share link → Accept this Quote → confirm() auto-accepted → green Quote Accepted block → reload still shows Accepted block (no button) → auto-created agreement /p/agreement/:id → button-method sign → public-signed-block → draft invoice INV-#### auto-created with quote_id+agreement_id linkage. All TEST_ artifacts cleaned up.
critical_code_review_comments: public_accept_quote runs auto-agreement creation synchronously (AI call ~10–30s). The Accept button on /p/quote/:id therefore hangs in spinner state for the full duration before the green block appears. Consider moving the agreement build to a BackgroundTask and returning 202 immediately; the public page can poll or just display Accepted optimistically (status is already flipped at the start of the handler).
server.py is now 2214 lines — the iteration_4 recommendation to split into routers still stands; the new public_accept_quote and auto-invoice logic inside public_sign_agreement compound the bloat.

Auto-invoice block inside public_sign_agreement is ~30 lines of business logic embedded in an API handler. Extract to a `_build_invoice_from_quote(user_id, quote, agreement_id)` helper for testability and reuse.

Invoice numbering uses `INV-{2000 + count + 1}` where count = total invoices for the user. Concurrent signings for the same user could race and produce duplicate numbers — consider an atomic counter or unique index.

public_accept_quote silently swallows AI agreement failures (returns ok:true even if agreement was not created). UI then will never see the agreement. Acceptable for resilience, but log severity/alerting should ensure these failures are surfaced.
seed_data_creation: Per-test TEST_ clients/quotes/agreements/invoices were created via fixtures and deleted in teardown. The E2E Playwright run also created and deleted: TEST_FE_AcceptClient_*, its quote, its auto-agreement, and its auto-invoice (INV-2003).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Closes loop on public client-side quote acceptance + auto-invoice on signing. Endpoints: POST /api/public/quotes/:id/accept (no auth) returns {ok:true} or {ok:true, already_approved:true}, rejects declined/converted (400) and missing (404). POST /api/public/agreements/:id/sign now also returns {ok:true, agreement:<updated>} and triggers a draft invoice with copied line_items when agreement.quote_id is set; idempotent via existing-invoice check on (user_id, quote_id). Frontend testids: accept-quote-btn, quote-accept-block, quote-accepted-block. NOTE: the public-accept handler is synchronous w/ AI (~10–30s) — use timeout >=60s when waiting for the green Quote Accepted block in Playwright.
rca of the issue: N/A — all flows behave as designed. Sole UX caveat is the ~10–30s spinner during public quote accept due to synchronous AI agreement generation; recommendation captured in code-review comments.
